title: Okay for a brief time but I wouldn’t try to go far
pros: Easy work, literally find the item, scan and go. It takes very minimal physical or mental effort.
cons: There’s no real guidance or training, and when you ask for help it’s rarely given. Managers talk horrible about employees right in front of and to other employees, and you’re made to feel like you have to “pick a side” often.
advice_to_management: Put more energy into actually managing shifts and less energy talking negatively about coworkers and creating a hostile environment. Managers should be the role models, not gossipers and instigators.

title: Good
pros: Pay is good and environment
cons: Commute far for me and

title: Low Pay, Overworked
pros: My coworkers were good and seemed to understand the job.  Our team could work as a unit very well.  The health insurance was fairly standard, but that was plenty.
cons: Very overworked and rushed, always ran on a skeleton crew - people would quit or be fired and not replaced.

title: Good relaxing job
pros: Relaxing, decent pay, flexible hours
cons: Management, coworkers can be hit/miss

title: Great place to work, not a ton of room to move around
pros: Great bonus and benefits minus the healthcare
cons:
Extremely corporate
Difficult to move around
Most roles are moving to Arkansas

title: Caring environment hindered by poor leadership and red tape
pros:
Meeting people
Being genuinely able to help your associates through struggles in their personal lives (death of a loved one, spouse moved out, utilities shut-off, etc...)
Annual MIP Bonus - Paid out in March, if employed thru the full month of January, last month of the fiscal year.
cons:
Work-life Balance is non-existent
"Stuff" rolls down-hill - at field level, you are the puppets for the 8 different puppeteers above you, that don't know what the puppeteers are doing. New direction seemingly every other day, or direction from above to redo something that was just done. 
Company speaks out of both sides of their mouths about supporting their Store Managers, wanting them to take PTO, etc.....
The Companies internal ethics and open-door process is designed to favor and protect the company (not a huge shock), and it absolutely runs that way.
No true "Ownership" over your own facility, but all the consequences.
Lots of red tape, lots of processes and daily activities that are over-complicated and sometimes unnecessary. Covid was awful, but it showed how well things can run when red-tape and barriers are removed and in this case, focus entirely on the Associates and Customers instead of worry about whatever metric is the flavor of the month, or whatever other nonsense they have you chasing.

title: Dont get stuck.
pros: easy to get hired and generally easy work.
cons: not worth sticking around, very limited room for promotion, tons of favoritism, no long term benefits, terrible yearly raises that don't keep up with inflation, awful management depending on your store.

title: Good co-workers, horrible management.
pros: Co-workers are good, benefits are above average for a retail store.
cons: Toxic management that creates a hostile work environment.
advice_to_management: If you are gonna become a manager learn how to do your job properly or simply put don’t become a damn manager. If you are gonna treat employees like garbage don’t become a manager. Walmart should start terminating managers who do such because all it does is make people wanna quit and increase turnover. I am talking to you Store Leads and Coaches if you don’t know how to do the job don’t sign up for it. Also shoutout team lead Jada and the other team leads who are wonderful.

title: Easy
pros: Coworkers were awesome to work with.
cons: Pay was a little too low to keep working there

title: Good place but political
pros: Teams are fast paced in certain areas and get to learn a lot about how retail works across supply chain and ecomm
cons: lots of politics trickles down and things may always be marked as an "emergency" or "urgent"

title: HRA
pros: Diverse workforce within stores and supply chain

title: Advertising
pros: A lot of growth opportunity

title: Generally easy job
pros: Walmart has generally good stock and 401k match programs (something like up to 6%). And depending on upper management it can be a straightforward easy job that’s enjoyable.
cons: It’s still a customer service job.

title: Ok for now

title: Walmart cashier Walmart
pros: Coworkers , flexible, good manager,
cons: Far away, location, unfriendly customers

title: What to Expect
pros: Employee 10% discount year round, One day of each month 20% discount
cons: The most Toxic, Dysfunctional Work Environment I have ever experienced in my 50 years of Retail. Treats customers as an inconvenience. No chance for advancement. No expectation or recognition for providing good customer service. Actually, management frowns upon employees taking time to help customers. It takes aways from time doing your job. Stocking shelves. Profiling and prejudice of customers and hiring employees by race and culture. Simply said, don't work there. Lowest pay in industry. Unless your management.
advice_to_management: Treat employees and customers as people.
